message:
feat(environment): Cx-first WeztermInfo::detect + DetectedEnvironment::detect (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first siblings to both public async detection methods.
Routes the `wezterm cli list-panes` subprocess call through
`list_panes_with_cx` (tick 27 WeztermInterface trait extension)
so caller cancellation propagates into the detection flow.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `WeztermInfo::detect_with_cx(cx, wezterm, shell)` — Cx-first
    parallel of `detect`. Swaps `handle.list_panes()` for
    `handle.list_panes_with_cx(cx)`. All other detection steps
    (version probe via `wezterm --version`, socket discovery)
    are pure-sync `std::process::Command` calls that don't use
    the async runtime — they fire regardless of cx state. The
    async seam is the `list_panes` call which can be slow on a
    loaded wezterm.

  * `DetectedEnvironment::detect_with_cx(cx, wezterm)` — routes
    through `WeztermInfo::detect_with_cx`. Shell / agent /
    remote / system detection are pure-sync and unchanged.

Behavioral preservation: for an uncancelled cx, the Cx-first
path is observationally equivalent to the legacy path. For a
cancelled cx, `list_panes_with_cx` surfaces the cancellation
via the wezterm trait's `_with_cx` semantics; the detect
function catches the error in the existing `match ... Err(_)`
arm and falls back to `list_ok = false` (same as a real
wezterm error). This produces a sensible default detection
result — cx cancellation doesn't corrupt the environment
snapshot.

Regression test (new)
`detect_with_cx_matches_legacy_with_mock_wezterm`:

  * Builds a MockWezterm with 1 "codex" pane.
  * Calls BOTH legacy `DetectedEnvironment::detect` and
    Cx-first `detect_with_cx` with the same handle.
  * Asserts:
    - agents.len() matches between paths
    - remotes.len() matches
    - system.os, system.arch, shell.shell_type identical
      (same host detection)

Uses raw `runtime.block_on` harness (matches tick 77/83/85
pattern) because environment::tests is all sync fixtures.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * environment:: — 66 passed / 0 failed (tick 85: 65 -> tick
    86: 66, +1 new parity test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (56.28s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Cx-first environment.rs trail COMPLETE — both public async
detection methods have cx siblings.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
